summary: sendgrid-sdk exfiltrates host and CI metadata to hardcoded IP on install
details: Typosquat of SendGrid's official @sendgrid/* packages. The package provides no real functionality (index.js exports an empty object) and exists only to run a postinstall beacon. On `npm install`, postinstall.js collects os.hostname(), the current working directory basename, the Node.js version, the CI flag, and a battery of CI environment variables that identify the installer's repository and internal infrastructure (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE_*, CI_PROJECT_PATH, BITBUCKET_REPO_FULL_NAME, BUILD_REPOSITORY_URI, TRAVIS_REPO_SLUG, JENKINS_URL, CI_SERVER_URL) plus the configured npm registry, and sends them as query-string parameters via `http.get` to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping — a bare IP over plain HTTP. The package's self-description as a 'security research honeypot' does not change the installer-side impact: any developer or CI system that installs this typosquat leaks internal repository slugs and CI server URLs to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
